name: BEND SURGERY CENTER
address: 1342 NE MEDICAL CENTER DR, BEND, OR 97701

fr.recommendations: Ensuring that all access points to the property are clearly marked and unobstructed could improve emergency responder access and facilitate efficient movement. Regular maintenance of vehicle parking areas to keep them free from obstacles may assist in swift ingress and egress for emergency vehicles. Considering the placement and design of landscaped areas so they do not encroach upon access pathways or vehicle movement zones would support effective operations by enhancing visibility and maneuverability. Evaluating the distribution and visibility of multiple access points, perhaps by keeping them well-lit and securely identifiable, may enhance responder navigation and staging, leading to more precise and coordinated emergency response efforts.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the property by increasing the separation between the vegetation and the structure could significantly reduce potential wildfire exposure. Managing and reducing vegetation directly adjacent to the structure may limit opportunities for fire to impact the building. For the vegetated perimeter, maintaining an open buffer by thinning and selectively clearing combustible vegetation can help preserve an effective break in fuel continuity. This approach may serve as a barrier to fire spread while supporting property preparedness. Additionally, addressing any trees adjacent to the parking area by creating space between tree canopies and maintaining lower limbs away from vehicles could improve access and safety for vehicles and individuals. These actions collectively enhance the defensible space, may promote firefighter access, and contribute to a more fire-resilient environment.
